name: Dutch-Apple Crunchcakes
recipeIngredient: 1 large Granny apple, cut into quarters
recipeInstructions:
-------------
TOPPING:
2/3 cup Flour
1/4 cup Light-brown sugar
1/2 stick Butter or Margarine
1/2 tsp Cinnamon
BATTER:
1 cup Flour
1/2 tsp Baking soda
1/2 tsp Cinnamon
1/4 tsp Salt
2/3 cup Sugar
1/2 cup Vegetable oil
1 Large Egg
1 tsp Vanilla extract
2 tablespoons (tb) Raisins
GARNISH:
1 cup Confectioners' sugar mixed with 2 teaspoons (tsp) water
Heat oven to 325°F. Grease and flour 12 regular-size muffin cups or line with paper liners. In a small bowl, mix apple pieces, sugar, and cinnamon. Stir topping ingredients in a small bowl with a fork or rub together with fingertips until large crumbs form.
BATTER: Whisk flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt in a small bowl until blended. Beat sugar into oil at high speed. Reduce speed to medium and beat in egg and vanilla. Drain liquid from apple (less than a teaspoon) and add to mixture. Reserve apple. Reduce speed to low and beat in flour mixture until blended. Batter will be very thick and look impossibly gooey. Beat in apple and raisins, breaking up apple a bit with the mixer. Spoon batter into prepared cups. Scatter topping mixture over batter and gently press into batter. Bake 30-35 minutes until crumbs are crisp and cupcakes are golden brown but still somewhat soft in the middle. Turn off oven. Leave cupcakes in oven for 5 minutes longer or until tops spring back when lightly pressed. Cool in pan on a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature, drizzled with confectioners' sugar mixture.
